---
title: "Verifying Document Metadata With The Snippet Viewer"
---

---
title: "Verifying Document Metadata with the Snippet Viewer"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Capture/pui1646248502604"
section: "Hyland Capture"
---

The Snippet Viewer is a rectangular floating component which is displayed above the Document Viewer. The Snippet Viewer enables you to view and navigate between each document metadata field and quickly verify each field. You can also draw a rectangle around the required text snipper and view the corresponding content on the Snippet Viewer. You can work with the Snipper Viewer even when the Document List and Metadata Panel are collapsed.

To verify document metadata with the Snippet Viewer:

1.  To change how the Snippet Viewer is displayed, do any of the following:
-
To drag the Snippet Viewer to a new position, click and hold the Drag snippet viewer icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
To collapse the Snippet Viewer, click the Collapse snippet viewer icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
To expand the Snippet Viewer, click the Expand snipper viewer icon in the lower-right corner of the Document Viewer.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  On the Snippet Viewer, navigate to the relevant metadata field which you want to verify and do one of the following:
-
In the document page, double-click the relevant text which you want to map to the corresponding metadata field.

-
Type the text which you want to map to the metadata field and then select it.

-
Draw a rectangle around the text which you want to map to the metadata field.

Note:
You can also navigate to the relevant metadata field on the Snippet Viewer by clicking the Ctrl and arrow keys. When you start to type, the auto-completion feature completes the word and suggests the best match among all of the words or candidates available. The auto-selected text also appears highlighted on the Document Viewer. However, multiple words are not supported during auto completion in extraction verification. To override auto-completion, continue typing the desired text or rubber band the whole word.

3.  Defocus the field by clicking outside it or pressing Enter or Tab. The field data is saved.
